input_row.title: Ensure 'SMB channel encryption' is set to 'AES-256-GCM' or higher for SMB file shares
input_row.description: Implement SMB channel encryption with AES-256-GCM for SMB file shares to ensure data confidentiality and integrity in transit. This method offers strong protection against eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks, safeguarding sensitive information.
input_row.rationale: AES-256-GCM encryption enhances the security of data transmitted over SMB channels by safeguarding it from unauthorized interception and tampering. Impact: Using the AES-256-GCM SMB channel encryption may impact client compatibility.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage accounts. 2. Click the name of a storage account. 3. Under Data storage, click File shares. 4. Under File share settings, click the link next to Security. 5. Under SMB channel encryption, ensure that AES-256-GCM, or higher, is the only checked SMB channel encryption setting. 6. Repeat steps 1-5 for each storage account. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list storage accounts: az storage account list For each storage account, run the following command: az storage account file-service-properties show --resource-group <resource- group> --account-name <storage-account> Ensure that under protocolSettings > smb, channelEncryption is set to AES-256- GCM;, or higher, only.  Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list storage accounts: Get-AzStorageAccount Run the following command to get the file service properties for a storage account in a resource group with a given name: $storageaccountfileservice = Get-AzStorageFileServiceProperty - ResourceGroupName <resource-group> -AccountName <storage-account> Run the following command to get the SMB channel encryption setting: $storageaccountfileservice.ProtocolSettings.Smb.ChannelEncryption Ensure that the command returns AES-256-GCM, or higher, only. Repeat for each storage account.
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Storage accounts. 2. Click the name of a storage account. 3. Under Data storage, click File shares. 4. Under File share settings, click the link next to Security. 5. If Profile is set to Maximum compatibility, click the drop-down menu and select Maximum security or Custom. 6. If selecting Custom, under SMB channel encryption, uncheck the boxes next to AES-128-CCM and AES-128-GCM. 7. Click Save. 8. Repeat steps 1-7 for each storage account requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each storage account requiring remediation, run the following command to set the SMB channel encryption: az storage account file-service-properties update --resource-group <resource- group> --account-name <storage-account> --channel-encryption AES-256-GCM Remediate from PowerShell For each storage account requiring remediation, run the following command to set the SMB channel encryption: Update-AzStorageFileServiceProperty -ResourceGroupName <resource-group> - StorageAccountName <storage-account> -SmbChannelEncryption AES-256-GCM Default Value: By default, the following SMB channel encryption algorithms are allowed: • AES-128-CCM • AES-128-GCM • AES-256-GCM References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/well-architected/service-guides/azure- files#recommendations-for-smb-file-shares 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/files/files-smb- protocol?tabs=azure-portal#smb-security-settings 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/storage/account/file-service-properties 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/get- azstoragefileserviceproperty 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.storage/update- azstoragefileserviceproperty
input_row.impact: Using the AES-256-GCM SMB channel encryption may impact client compatibility.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit approach is classified as Automated due to several factors:
- The audit field specifies clear Azure CLI and PowerShell commands that can be used to check the setting for SMB channel encryption.
- The control pertains to a standard Azure resource property related to encryption settings, which can be accessed via Azure APIs.
- As per the description and rationale, this control is focused on configurations that are typically exposed through Azure Resource Manager APIs.
  
**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_storage_file_share_smb_channel_encryption_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the SMB channel encryption setting for Azure file shares by executing the following actions:

1. **Resource Enumeration**:
   - Enumerate all Azure storage accounts in the subscription or specified resource group using:
     - Azure CLI command: `az storage account list --query "[].{Name:name, ResourceGroup:resourceGroup}"` 
     - PowerShell command: `Get-AzStorageAccount`

2. **Check SMB Channel Encryption**:
   - For each storage account, retrieve file service properties to check the SMB channel encryption setting using:
     - Azure CLI command: `az storage account file-service-properties show --resource-group <resource-group> --account-name <storage-account>`
     - PowerShell command: `$storageaccountfileservice = Get-AzStorageFileServiceProperty -ResourceGroupName <resource-group> -AccountName <storage-account>`
   - Validate that the `protocolSettings > smb > channelEncryption` property is set to `AES-256-GCM` or higher.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - The configuration passes if the `channelEncryption` value is exactly `AES-256-GCM` or higher.
   - The configuration fails if any other value (e.g., `AES-128-CCM` or `AES-128-GCM`) is found.

4. **API Endpoints/Commands**:
   - The endpoints used are part of the Azure Storage service through Azure Management APIs for checking file service properties.
   - Example of the API endpoint that may be used would relate to the file service property retrievals in Azure, ensuring compliance with the encryption requirements.

By implementing this automated approach, compliance with the control can be efficiently verified across all relevant storage accounts.
